title: buxus japonica var. microphylla
intro: Buxus japonica var. microphylla, also known as Japanese boxwood or small-leaf boxwood, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Buxaceae. It is native to China, Japan, and Southeast Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, and is often used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Buxus japonica var. microphylla are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
